solution: 
```class Solution:
    def validSequence(self, word1: str, word2: str) -> List[int]:
        m, n = len(word1), len(word2)
        if m - n > 1: return []
        diff = sum(c1 != c2 for c1, c2 in zip(word1, word2))
        if diff > 1: return []
        if diff == 0: return list(range(n))
        idx = [i for i, (c1, c2) in enumerate(zip(word1, word2)) if c1 != c2]
        if diff == 1 and idx[0] != 0 and idx[0] != n - 1:
            return [0] + sorted(idx + [n - 1])
        return sorted(idx)
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution first checks if the difference between the lengths of the two words is more than 1, in which case it returns an empty list. Then it counts the number of different characters between the two words. If the difference is more than 1, it returns an empty list. If the difference is 0, it returns a list of indices from 0 to n-1. If the difference is 1, it finds the indices of the different characters and returns the lexicographically smallest valid sequence of indices.
